name: Whole Wheat Baguettes
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons Light or dark brown sugar
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, dissolve yeast, brown sugar, and ginger in warm water.
Let stand 5 minutes until yeast foams.
2. Add salt and bread flour; beat well. Stir in whole wheat flour to make a stiff dough. Turn out onto lightly floured board and knead 10 minutes or until dough is springy, smooth, and satiny.
3. Wash bowl with water and grease it. Add dough to bowl; turn over to grease top. Let rise in a warm place until doubled in bulk, about 1 hour.
4. Lightly oil or grease a 14x17-inch baking sheet and sprinkle with cornmeal. Set aside.
5. Punch down the dough. Divide in half and, on a lightly floured surface, shape into long loaves, rolling each piece first into a 5x12-inch rectangle, then rolling each up along the long side to make a long, narrow loaf. Pinch ends to seal.
6. Place loaves, seam side down, on prepared baking sheet. Make 4-5 slashes across each loaf with sharp knife or razor blade.
7. Let rise until doubled, about 45 minutes. Brush each loaf with water and sprinkle with whole wheat flour.
8. Bake in a preheated 400°F oven for 15-20 minutes, until loaves are browned and crisp; remove to a wire rack to cool completely.
Makes 2 loaves; serves 16.
